title: Another index table delete mutation stuck
description:
### Describe the bug
Not sure if real issue again. Reproducible is different from https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/issues/79123
### How to reproduce
Run:
```sql
CREATE TABLE t0 (c0 LowCardinality(UInt128), INDEX i0 90 % c0 TYPE hypothesis, c1 Bool, c2 Map(Int64,Int64)) ENGINE = MergeTree() PRIMARY KEY tuple();
SET min_insert_block_size_bytes = 32, optimize_trivial_insert_select = 1, exclude_materialize_skip_indexes_on_insert = 'i0';
INSERT INTO TABLE t0 (c1, c2, c0) SELECT c1, c2, c0 FROM generateRandom('c1 Bool, c2 Map(Int64,Int64), c0 LowCardinality(UInt128)', 54, 1, 1) LIMIT 5;
INSERT INTO TABLE t0 (c0, c2, c1) SELECT CAST(number AS UInt128), map(CAST((-number) AS Int64),1, CAST(number AS Int64),CAST(number AS Int64)), TRUE FROM numbers(5);
DELETE FROM t0 WHERE c1; --stuck
```
### Error message and/or stacktrace
Getting this division by 0 error on a loop:
```
<Error> MergeTreeBackgroundExecutor: Exception while executing background task {50f34b5c-ccd5-416a-88b7-9917a407479e::all_1_6_1}: Code: 153. DB::Exception: Division by zero: while executing 'FUNCTION modulo(90 :: 1, c0 :: 0) -> modulo(90, c0) LowCardinality(UInt128) : 3'. (ILLEGAL_DIVISION), Stack trace (when copying this message, always include the lines below):
0. contrib/llvm-project/libcxx/include/__exception/exception.h:113: Poco::Exception::Exception(String const&, int) @ 0x0000000024ab61f2
1. src/Common/Exception.cpp:129: DB::Exception::Exception(DB::Exception::MessageMasked&&, int, bool) @ 0x0000000014fc97e9
2. src/Common/Exception.h:123: DB::Exception::Exception(String&&, int, String, bool) @ 0x000000000d55fcce
3. src/Common/Exception.h:58: DB::Exception::Exception(PreformattedMessage&&, int) @ 0x000000000d55f6d1
4. src/Common/Exception.h:141: DB::Exception::Exception<>(int, FormatStringHelperImpl<>) @ 0x000000000d56c6f6
5. src/Functions/DivisionUtils.h:26: wide::integer<128ul, unsigned int> DB::ModuloImpl<char8_t, wide::integer<128ul, unsigned int>>::apply<wide::integer<128ul, unsigned int>>(char8_t, wide::integer<128ul, unsigned int>) @ 0x0000000011c8eee7
6. src/Functions/FunctionBinaryArithmetic.h:325: void DB::impl_::BinaryOperation<char8_t, wide::integer<128ul, unsigned int>, DB::ModuloImpl<char8_t, wide::integer<128ul, unsigned int>>, wide::integer<128ul, unsigned int>>::process<(DB::impl_::OpCase)1>(char8_t const*, wide::integer<128ul, unsigned int> const*, wide::integer<128ul, unsigned int>*, unsigned long, DB::PODArray<char8_t, 4096ul, Allocator<false, false>, 63ul, 64ul> const*) @ 0x0000000011c8ecc0
7. src/Functions/FunctionBinaryArithmetic.h:2498: COW<DB::IColumn>::immutable_ptr<DB::IColumn> DB::FunctionBinaryArithmetic<DB::ModuloImpl, DB::NameModulo, false, true, false>::executeNumeric<DB::DataTypeNumber<char8_t>, DB::DataTypeNumber<wide::integer<128ul, unsigned int>>>(std::vector<DB::ColumnWithTypeAndName, std::allocator<DB::ColumnWithTypeAndName>> const&, DB::DataTypeNumber<char8_t> const&, DB::DataTypeNumber<wide::integer<128ul, unsigned int>> const&, DB::PODArray<char8_t, 4096ul, Allocator<false, false>, 63ul, 64ul> const*) const @ 0x0000000011ed049e
8. src/Functions/FunctionBinaryArithmetic.h:2693: bool DB::FunctionBinaryArithmetic<DB::ModuloImpl, DB::NameModulo, false, true, false>::castType<auto bool DB::FunctionBinaryArithmetic<DB::ModuloImpl, DB::NameModulo, false, true, false>::castBothTypes<DB::FunctionBinaryArithmetic<DB::ModuloImpl, DB::NameModulo, false, true, false>::executeImpl2(std::vector<DB::ColumnWithTypeAndName, std::allocator<DB::ColumnWithTypeAndName>> const&, std::shared_ptr<DB::IDataType const> const&, unsigned long, DB::PODArray<char8_t, 4096ul, Allocator<false, false>, 63ul, 64ul> const*) const::'lambda'(auto const&, auto const&)>(DB::IDataType const*, DB::IDataType const*, auto&&)::'lambda'(auto const&)::operator()<DB::DataTypeNumber<char8_t>>(auto const&) const::'lambda'(auto const&)>(DB::IDataType const*, auto&&) @ 0x0000000011ecd608

```
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1 (c0 UInt32, c1 VARCHAR(255), c2 DOUBLE PRECISION);
INSERT INTO t1 (c0, c1, c2) VALUES (1, 'test', 1.5), (2, 'example', 2.5), (3, 'sample', 3.5);
CREATE TABLE t2 (c0 BIGINT, c1 INT, c2 TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO t2 (c0, c1, c2) SELECT number, NULL, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP FROM generate_series(0, 4);
key_bug_features: DELETE operation on table with specific index configuration
